senses.definition: a structure that has walls, a roof, and sometimes many levels, like an apartment, house, school, etc.

examples.x: She lives on the third floor of a residential building.
The school building has a playground for the students.
The tall building stands out among the other structures in the city.
The workers construct the building from the ground up.

senses.definition: a building where people live, especially as a family

examples.x: She invited her friends over to her house for a birthday party.
The house has a basement where they store their belongings.
We painted our house a vibrant shade of blue to stand out in the neighborhood.
He invited friends to his house for dinner.
The modern house featured large windows, allowing ample natural light to fill every room.

senses.definition: the place that we live in, usually with our family

examples.x: He enjoys the peaceful atmosphere of his home.
He has a cozy corner in his home where he can meditate.
He missed his home while traveling and couldn't wait to be back.
She enjoys cooking meals for her family in the kitchen of their home.
The home is a place where you can be yourself and feel loved.

senses.definition: a place that has a few rooms for people to live in, normally part of a building that has other such places on each floor

examples.x: He rented an apartment in the city to be closer to his workplace.
He uses the apartment's laundry facilities to wash his clothes.
She invited her friends over to her apartment for a movie night.
She lives in a small apartment on the third floor of the building.
The apartment has a secure entry system.

senses.definition: the bottom of a room that we walk on

examples.x: He swept the floor to remove the dust and dirt.
My child sits on the floor to play with his toys.
She accidentally dropped a plate, and it shattered into pieces on the floor.
She covered the floor with a colorful carpet.
She spilled juice on the floor and immediately cleaned it up.

senses.definition: the thing we move to enter, exit, or access a place such as a vehicle, building, room, etc.

examples.x: He closed the door behind him as he entered the room.
He held the door open for an elderly person entering the building.
He locked the door before leaving the house.
She closed the bedroom door to have some privacy.
She knocked on the door and waited for someone to answer.

senses.definition: a space in a wall or vehicle that is made of glass and we use to look outside or get some fresh air

examples.x: The gentle breeze flowed through the open window, bringing the scent of blooming flowers.
She looked out of the window and saw a rainbow in the distance.
She opened the window to let in some fresh air.
She waved to her friend from the window as they walked by.
The window had a transparent glass that allowed sunlight to pass through.

senses.definition: an upright structure, usually made of brick, concrete, or stone that is made to divide, protect, or surround a place

examples.x: He noticed a spider crawling up the wall.
He placed a bookshelf against the wall to store his books.
He stands on a ladder to reach the top of the wall for painting.
She hangs a painting on the wall to add color to the room.
She placed a calendar on the wall to keep track of important dates.

senses.definition: a space in a building with walls, a floor, and a ceiling where people do different activities

examples.x: I have a big room with a window.
My favorite room in the house is the kitchen because I love cooking.
There is a small room next to the living room for reading.
Please keep your room clean and organized.
We painted my room blue to make it feel more relaxing.
I found a quiet room to study for my exams.

senses.definition: the structure that creates the outer top part of a vehicle, building, etc.

examples.x: He placed solar panels on the roof to generate electricity.
She climbed onto the roof to enjoy the view from above.
She noticed a small bird nesting on the roof of her garage.
She sat on the roof of her car to enjoy a picnic under the stars.
The snow on the roof started to melt in the warmth of the sun.

senses.definition: the highest part of a room, vehicle, etc. that covers it from the inside

examples.x: He stood on a ladder to change a lightbulb on the ceiling.
He installed soundproofing material on the ceiling to reduce noise from the floor above.
He noticed a water stain on the ceiling and called a professional to fix the leak.
He used a long brush to paint the high ceiling of the living room.
She lies on the floor, imagining shapes on the ceiling.

senses.definition: the part of a house where people spend time together talking, watching television, relaxing, etc.

examples.x: She gathered with her family in the living room to play board games.
She sat at the dining table in the living room and enjoyed a meal.
The living room had a comfortable sofa where she took afternoon naps.
A vibrant rug added a pop of color to the living room, complementing the tasteful decor.
In the living room, family and friends gathered for laughter and shared stories during the holidays.

senses.definition: a room that we use to eat meals in

examples.x: She cleans the dining room after each meal.
She set the table with plates and glasses in the dining room.
They enjoyed breakfast in the dining room, starting the day with a tasty meal.
They enjoyed lively conversations over dinner in the cozy dining room.
They gathered in the dining room for Sunday brunch.

senses.definition: the place in a building or home where we make food

examples.x: My mother believes that no one should leave the kitchen after a meal until they have completely cleaned it.
She cooked a delicious meal in the kitchen.
She stored canned goods and snacks in the kitchen pantry.
The kitchen had a double oven, allowing for simultaneous baking of different dishes.
The mother asked her children to leave the kitchen until she finished preparing dinner.

senses.definition: a room we use for sleeping

examples.x: He kept his favorite toys on the shelves in his bedroom.
In our house, the bigger bedroom was always reserved for my older sister.
My siblings and I share a single bedroom in our house.
She decorated the walls of her bedroom with pictures and posters.
She placed a small nightstand next to the bed in the bedroom for her belongings.

senses.definition: a room that has a toilet and a sink, and often times a bathtub or a shower as well

examples.x: He took a refreshing shower in the bathroom after a long day.
She brushed her teeth in the bathroom sink before going to bed.
He cleans the bathroom regularly to keep it hygienic and tidy.
They organized shampoo and soap bottles in the shower caddy in the bathroom.
She used a hairdryer in the bathroom to dry her hai.

senses.definition: the land that is joined to our house and we can grow plants there

examples.x: I like to take walks in the garden to admire the plants and listen to the birds.
The garden is my favorite place to have a cup of tea in the morning.
We decorate the garden with colorful lights during the holiday season.
We have a small garden in our backyard where we grow tomatoes and herbs.
We often have family gatherings in the garden during summer evenings.

senses.definition: on or toward a higher part of a building

examples.x: I prefer to sleep upstairs in the loft rather than downstairs.
I went upstairs to grab my phone from my bedroom.
Let's have a movie night upstairs in the entertainment room.
Please keep the noise down, our neighbors upstairs might get disturbed.
The children were playing upstairs in their room.

senses.definition: on or toward a lower part of a building, particularly the first floor

examples.x: I left my bag downstairs in the entryway.
I prefer to eat breakfast downstairs in the dining room.
She lives downstairs in the basement apartment.
We have a cozy sitting area downstairs by the fireplace.
We have a home gym downstairs for exercising and staying fit.

senses.definition: a small space or room built into a wall, which is used to store things and is usually shelved

examples.x: She stored her clothes and shoes neatly in the closet.
The closet had a light that automatically turned on when the door was opened.
In the closet, she discovered a collection of vintage dresses and nostalgic memories.
A mysterious noise echoed from the closet, sparking her curiosity and a hint of apprehension.
His favorite childhood toys were hidden away in the closet, waiting for the next generation.

senses.definition: any of the pieces making a whole, when combined

examples.x: Asking questions is an integral part of gaining knowledge.
The backyard is a private part of the house.
The living room is my favorite part of the house because it's where we relax and spend time together.
The screen is the main part of a laptop.

senses.definition: a box-like device that moves up and down and is used to get to the different levels of a building

examples.x: I felt a bit nervous when the elevator suddenly stopped between floors.
I prefer taking the elevator in tall buildings rather than using the stairs.
I pressed the button and waited for the elevator to arrive.
It's important to hold the elevator door open if someone is rushing to catch it.
We took the elevator to the top floor of the building.

senses.definition: the land joined to our house where we can grow grass, flowers, and other plants

examples.x: I saw a squirrel running across the yard.
Our dog loves running around in the yard.
The children played games in the yard.
We set up a swing set in the yard.
